mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer price indices indicate robust consumer spending outpacing supply constraints, which boosts corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL rises faster than RSAFS, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and improving supply chain throughput, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat forward returns when global shipping indices remain elevated, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 70 percent of supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer discretionary spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth consistently coincides with negative 20-day forward returns, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.0 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and declining consumer confidence in long-term wealth, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of months where HOUST drops below 1000, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for mortgage rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a lagging federal funds rate creates a negative real interest rate environment that forces markets to price in aggressive future tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts positive returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 8.0 while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0, or the interaction effect reverses sign during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent confirms strong macroeconomic momentum that lifts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during early-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 3.0 percent coincides with negative forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations, or the predictive power vanishes when real interest rates are above 2.5 percent.
